text:
COMPREHENSIVE SOMATIC NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT
Repeat Tumor Profiling — Resistance Evaluation

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Patient Name:           Raymond Kowalski
Date of Birth:          [DOB on file]
Medical Record Number:  [MRN on file]
Ordering Physician:     [Treating Oncologist]
Diagnosis:              Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC),
                        adenocarcinoma with emergent neuroendocrine features,
                        Gleason score 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5
Prior Treatments:       ADT (leuprolide) + abiraterone/prednisone (first-line mHSPC);
                        enzalutamide (second-line, CRPC); olaparib 300 mg BID →
                        200 mg BID (third-line, CRPC; germline/somatic BRCA2)
                        Ongoing ADT continuation throughout
Clinical Indication:    Biochemical and radiographic progression on olaparib;
                        new visceral metastases (liver, lung); evaluate for
                        acquired resistance mechanisms

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Specimen ID:            [Lab accession number on file]
Specimen Type:          Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue — CT-guided
                        core needle biopsy of right hepatic lobe metastasis
Procedure Date:         [Date on file]
Date Received:          [Date on file]
Date Reported:          [Date on file]
Tumor Content (Pathologist Estimate): 65% (confirmed by H&E review prior to
                        DNA extraction; necrosis approximately 10%)
Platform:               Hybrid capture-based comprehensive genomic profiling,
                        364-gene panel (DNA) + whole-transcriptome RNA fusion panel
Sequencing Coverage:    Mean unique coverage 650x (DNA); tumor mutational burden
                        and microsatellite instability assessed from DNA sequencing
Germline Reference:     Prior germline testing result (blood-based) available for
                        somatic/germline variant deconvolution

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QUALITY METRICS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DNA Quality:            Adequate (DIN equivalent ≥4; ≥50 ng input DNA)
On-target Uniformity:  94.1% bases covered at ≥100x
Tumor Fraction (in silico estimate): 0.68
RNA Quality:            Adequate (DV200 62%)
QC Result:             PASS — all quality thresholds met

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY OF FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This repeat comprehensive genomic profiling of a hepatic metastasis demonstrates
several clinically important acquired alterations compared to the baseline somatic
NGS performed at initial diagnosis on the primary prostate biopsy specimen.
Key findings include:
  1. Retained biallelic BRCA2 loss (no reversion mutations detected)
  2. Newly acquired androgen receptor (AR) amplification with concurrent AR
     ligand-binding domain missense mutation (p.L702H) — consistent with AR
     pathway re-engagement as a dominant resistance mechanism
  3. Newly acquired RB1 homozygous deletion (previously heterozygous)
  4. MYC amplification markedly increased (copy number 18, up from 6)
  5. Newly acquired PIK3CA p.H1047R activating mutation
  6. Increased TP53 variant allele fraction consistent with clonal evolution
  7. No BRCA2 reversion mutations detected — olaparib resistance is
     AR-pathway and non-reversion mediated in this case

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DETAILED GENOMIC FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 1 — PATHOGENIC / LIKELY PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS (CLINICALLY ACTIONABLE
             OR PROGNOSTICALLY SIGNIFICANT)

┌──────────────┬───────────────────────────────┬──────────┬────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┐
│ Gene         │ Alteration                    │ VAF / CN │ Status     │ Clinical Significance             │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ BRCA2        │ c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22 │ VAF 82%  │ Somatic    │ Biallelic loss retained; germline │
│              │ (frameshift, loss-of-function)│          │ (germline  │ pathogenic variant + somatic LOH  │
│              │                               │          │ + somatic  │ maintained; PARP inhibitor class  │
│              │                               │          │ LOH)       │ sensitivity predicted absent of   │
│              │                               │          │            │ reversion mutation                │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ AR           │ Amplification                 │ CN = 12  │ Somatic,   │ Newly acquired; AR amplification  │
│              │ (high-level)                  │ (gain)   │ acquired   │ is the most common mechanism of   │
│              │                               │          │            │ ARPI resistance in mCRPC; drives  │
│              │                               │          │            │ AR pathway re-engagement despite  │
│              │                               │          │            │ prior enzalutamide and olaparib   │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ AR           │ p.L702H (c.2105T>A)           │ VAF 22%  │ Somatic,   │ Newly acquired ligand-binding     │
│              │ (missense, LBD mutation)      │          │ acquired   │ domain mutation; confers agonist  │
│              │                               │          │            │ activity to AR with glucocorticoid│
│              │                               │          │            │ ligands (prednisone); known       │
│              │                               │          │            │ resistance mechanism to           │
│              │                               │          │            │ enzalutamide; may partially        │
│              │                               │          │            │ modulate olaparib+ARPI responses  │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ TP53         │ p.R248W (c.742C>T)            │ VAF 55%  │ Somatic    │ Hotspot gain-of-function mutation;│
│              │ (missense, gain-of-function)  │          │ (retained, │ VAF increased from 42% at         │
│              │                               │          │ clonal     │ diagnosis, indicating clonal      │
│              │                               │          │ expansion) │ expansion of TP53-mutant subclone │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ PTEN         │ Homozygous deletion           │ CN = 0   │ Somatic    │ Retained from prior testing;      │
│              │                               │          │ (retained) │ PI3K/AKT pathway activation;      │
│              │                               │          │            │ may contribute to combined        │
│              │                               │          │            │ PI3K pathway dysregulation with   │
│              │                               │          │            │ newly acquired PIK3CA p.H1047R    │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ RB1          │ Homozygous deletion           │ CN = 0   │ Somatic,   │ Newly biallelic (previously       │
│              │                               │          │ acquired   │ heterozygous deletion); RB1       │
│              │                               │          │ (now       │ homozygous loss is associated     │
│              │                               │          │ biallelic) │ with aggressive CRPC, ARPI        │
│              │                               │          │            │ resistance, and propensity for    │
│              │                               │          │            │ neuroendocrine transdifferentiation│
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ MYC          │ Amplification                 │ CN = 18  │ Somatic    │ Markedly increased from CN 6 at   │
│              │                               │          │ (markedly  │ diagnosis; high-level MYC         │
│              │                               │          │ increased) │ amplification associated with     │
│              │                               │          │            │ aggressive disease, cell cycle     │
│              │                               │          │            │ dysregulation, and metabolic      │
│              │                               │          │            │ reprogramming                     │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ PIK3CA       │ p.H1047R (c.3140A>G)          │ VAF 18%  │ Somatic,   │ Newly acquired activating hotspot │
│              │ (missense, activating)        │          │ acquired   │ mutation in PI3K catalytic subunit│
│              │                               │          │            │ alpha; in context of PTEN loss,   │
│              │                               │          │            │ dual PI3K pathway activation;     │
│              │                               │          │            │ potentially targetable (PI3K/AKT/ │
│              │                               │          │            │ mTOR inhibitors under study in    │
│              │                               │          │            │ prostate cancer)                  │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ CDK4         │ Amplification                 │ CN = 8   │ Somatic    │ Retained and increased from prior;│
│              │                               │          │ (increased)│ CDK4/6 pathway activation; cell   │
│              │                               │          │            │ cycle G1/S checkpoint disruption  │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ CCND1        │ Gain                          │ CN = 4   │ Somatic,   │ Newly detected CCND1 (Cyclin D1)  │
│              │                               │          │ acquired   │ gain; amplifies CDK4 pathway      │
│              │                               │          │            │ dysregulation; cell cycle          │
│              │                               │          │            │ accelerator                       │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ FOXA1        │ p.R265Q (c.794G>A)            │ VAF 33%  │ Somatic    │ Retained pioneer transcription    │
│              │ (missense)                    │          │ (retained) │ factor mutation; regulates AR     │
│              │                               │          │            │ chromatin accessibility           │
├──────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
│ SPOP         │ p.F133V (c.397T>G)            │ VAF 16%  │ Somatic    │ Retained; E3 ubiquitin ligase     │
│              │ (missense)                    │          │ (retained) │ substrate adaptor mutation;        │
│              │                               │          │            │ co-occurring with FOXA1 mutation  │
└──────────────┴───────────────────────────────┴──────────┴────────────┴───────────────────────────────────┘

SECTION 2 — VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)

┌──────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Gene         │ Alteration                       │ VAF      │ Notes                                    │
├──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ KDM6A        │ p.T408A (c.1222A>G)              │ VAF 8%   │ Histone demethylase; epigenetic          │
│              │ missense                         │          │ regulator; VUS in prostate context;      │
│              │                                  │          │ low VAF suggests minor subclone          │
├──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ NCOR1        │ p.R1181H (c.3542G>A)             │ VAF 12%  │ Nuclear corepressor 1; VUS; involved in  │
│              │ missense                         │          │ AR transcriptional repression; may        │
│              │                                  │          │ contribute to AR activity                │
├──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ZBTB16       │ Focal deletion (heterozygous)    │ CN = 1   │ PLZF transcription factor; VUS;          │
│              │                                  │          │ reported in treatment-resistant prostate │
│              │                                  │          │ cancer; significance uncertain           │
└──────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┴──────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

SECTION 3 — GENES WITH NO PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS DETECTED (SELECTED)

The following genes were sequenced and no pathogenic or likely pathogenic
alterations were identified:
  - BRCA1 (wild-type)
  - ATM (wild-type — consistent with prior testing)
  - CDK12 (wild-type — consistent with prior testing)
  - CHEK2 (wild-type — consistent with prior testing)
  - MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 (all wild-type; MSI-stable confirmed)
  - PALB2 (wild-type)
  - RAD51C, RAD51D (wild-type)
  - BRIP1 (wild-type)
  - APC (wild-type)
  - CTNNB1 (wild-type)
  - NOTCH1, NOTCH2 (wild-type)
  - WNT pathway genes: no activating mutations detected
  - ERG, ETV1, ETV4 (no ETS fusions detected; TMPRSS2-ERG fusion absent,
    consistent with prior result)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BRCA2 REVERSION MUTATION ANALYSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Clinical Question: Are secondary (reversion) mutations restoring BRCA2 open
reading frame present, which would predict resistance to PARP inhibitors?

Result: NO BRCA2 REVERSION MUTATIONS DETECTED

Analysis: The index BRCA2 frameshift mutation (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22)
remains present at high VAF (82%), with no secondary intragenic mutations or
deletions detected that would restore the wild-type BRCA2 reading frame.
Extensive deep sequencing was performed across BRCA2 exons 11–27 (the mutation
locus and flanking sequence) at a minimum coverage of 1,000x to maximize
sensitivity for low-frequency reversion events. Additionally, no copy-neutral
reversion events (e.g., gene conversion) were detected by allele-specific
analysis.

Interpretation: Olaparib resistance in this patient does not appear to be
mediated by BRCA2 restoration. The dominant resistance mechanism is most
consistent with AR pathway re-engagement (AR amplification + AR p.L702H LBD
mutation), with concurrent loss of RB1 homozygosity potentially contributing
to phenotypic plasticity and neuroendocrine transdifferentiation.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FUSION ANALYSIS (RNA-BASED)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AR-V7 Splice Variant:          DETECTED — AR-V7 splice variant mRNA expression
                                present (supports AR pathway re-activation; NOTE:
                                RNA-based detection from tissue, not ctDNA)
TMPRSS2-ERG:                   NOT DETECTED (consistent with prior result)
TMPRSS2-ETV1, -ETV4, -ETV5:   NOT DETECTED
Other Actionable Fusions:       NONE DETECTED (NTRK1/2/3, RET, ALK, ROS1,
                                FGFR1/2/3/4 fusions not detected)
SYNAPTOPHYSIN / Neuroendocrine Gene Expression: Elevated relative expression
                                of CHGA (chromogranin A), SYP (synaptophysin),
                                and NCAM1 (CD56) transcripts detected above
                                reference range for prostate adenocarcinoma,
                                consistent with emergent neuroendocrine
                                transdifferentiation (correlates with
                                pathologic IHC findings)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TMB:                            5 mutations per megabase (mutations/Mb)
Classification:                 LOW (threshold for high TMB: ≥10 mutations/Mb)
Interpretation:                 TMB-low; pembrolizumab (Keytruda) based on
                                TMB-H indication is NOT applicable in this case.
                                Marginal increase from 3 mutations/Mb at
                                baseline may reflect modest mutational
                                accumulation with therapy.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MSI Status:                     MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Loci Analyzed:                  114 microsatellite loci examined
Interpretation:                 MSI-H/dMMR not present; pembrolizumab based
                                on MSI-H/dMMR indication is NOT applicable.
                                MMR gene analysis (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2)
                                confirmed no pathogenic variants.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMPARATIVE GENOMIC EVOLUTION — PRIOR vs. CURRENT SEQUENCING
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This specimen was compared to the somatic NGS performed at initial diagnosis
(primary prostate biopsy, 84-gene panel). The following changes are noted:

NEWLY ACQUIRED ALTERATIONS (not present at diagnosis):
  + AR amplification (CN 12) — NEWLY ACQUIRED [RESISTANCE DRIVER]
  + AR p.L702H LBD mutation (VAF 22%) — NEWLY ACQUIRED [RESISTANCE DRIVER]
  + RB1 homozygous deletion (CN 0; previously heterozygous, CN 1) — ACQUIRED
  + PIK3CA p.H1047R (VAF 18%) — NEWLY ACQUIRED
  + CCND1 gain (CN 4) — NEWLY ACQUIRED
  + AR-V7 splice variant (RNA) — NEWLY DETECTED

RETAINED ALTERATIONS (present at diagnosis and retained):
  = BRCA2 c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22 (VAF 78% → 82%) — CLONALLY RETAINED
  = TP53 p.R248W (VAF 42% → 55%) — EXPANDED
  = PTEN homozygous deletion — RETAINED
  = MYC gain/amplification (CN 6 → 18) — MARKEDLY AMPLIFIED
  = CDK4 amplification (CN increased) — RETAINED, AMPLIFIED
  = FOXA1 p.R265Q (VAF 31% → 33%) — RETAINED
  = SPOP p.F133V (VAF 18% → 16%) — RETAINED

NO LONGER DETECTED:
  — No alterations detected at prior assessment appear to have been lost,
    suggesting stable clonal architecture with superimposed acquired
    resistance alterations.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATHWAY-LEVEL SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AR SIGNALING PATHWAY:          HIGHLY ACTIVE — AR amplification (CN 12),
                                AR p.L702H LBD mutation, AR-V7 splice variant,
                                and FOXA1 p.R265Q collectively indicate robust
                                AR pathway re-engagement

DNA DAMAGE RESPONSE (DDR):     BRCA2 biallelic loss retained; no reversion;
                                ATM, CDK12, CHEK2 wild-type; homologous
                                recombination deficiency (HRD) persists
                                despite PARP inhibitor exposure

PI3K/AKT/mTOR PATHWAY:        HIGHLY DYSREGULATED — PTEN homozygous deletion
                                + newly acquired PIK3CA p.H1047R; convergent
                                dual mechanism PI3K pathway activation

CELL CYCLE / PROLIFERATION:    MARKEDLY DYSREGULATED — RB1 biallelic loss,
                                CDK4 amplification, CCND1 gain, MYC high-level
                                amplification, TP53 gain-of-function; consistent
                                with loss of G1/S checkpoint control

NEUROENDOCRINE FEATURES:       RB1 and TP53 co-alteration (known genomic
                                substrate for lineage plasticity and NEPC
                                transdifferentiation); elevated NE gene
                                expression (CHGA, SYP, NCAM1) on RNA panel;
                                correlates with histologic focal NE
                                differentiation on concurrent biopsy

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PHARMACOGENOMIC SUMMARY — THERAPIES WITH POTENTIAL RELEVANCE
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTE: The following is a genomic pharmacogenomic summary only. Therapeutic
decisions should be made by the treating oncologist in the context of full
clinical data, current standard of care, and available clinical trial
options. This report does not constitute a treatment recommendation.

PARP Inhibitors (olaparib, rucaparib, niraparib, talazoparib):
  - BRCA2 biallelic loss retained; no reversion mutations
  - Continued BRCA2 loss is a predictive biomarker for HRD/PARP inhibitor
    sensitivity; however, the dominant acquired resistance mechanisms (AR
    amplification, AR-V7, AR LBD mutation) suggest AR pathway escape as the
    primary mechanism of clinical progression rather than loss of HRD
  - AR pathway co-targeting may be required; lutetium-177 PSMA or other
    approaches also potentially applicable pending PSMA expression

AR Pathway Agents (enzalutamide, apalutamide, darolutamide, abiraterone):
  - AR amplification + AR p.L702H + AR-V7 collectively predict resistance
    to available AR pathway inhibitors as single agents
  - Novel AR degraders (ARV-110/bavdegalutamide) may retain activity
    against LBD-mutant AR; investigational

PI3K/AKT Pathway Inhibitors (ipatasertib, capivasertib, copanlisib):
  - PTEN loss + PIK3CA p.H1047R dual activation: strong preclinical rationale
    for PI3K/AKT inhibition; capivasertib (AKT inhibitor) in combination with
    abiraterone under evaluation in prostate cancer

CDK4/6 Inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib):
  - CDK4 amplification + CCND1 gain + RB1 loss: RB1 biallelic deletion
    predicts resistance to CDK4/6 inhibitors (RB1 protein loss abrogates
    the on-target mechanism); CDK4/6 inhibitors NOT predicted to be
    beneficial in this context

Platinum-Based Chemotherapy:
  - BRCA2 biallelic loss + RB1/TP53 co-alteration (neuroendocrine features):
    both HRD-related platinum sensitivity and standard NEPC treatment
    protocols support platinum-based regimens (e.g., carboplatin/etoposide)

Immunotherapy (PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors, pembrolizumab):
  - MSS, TMB-low: immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy unlikely to provide
    benefit based on current biomarker criteria; CDK12 wild-type (no
    tandem duplicator phenotype)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERPRETATION AND CONCLUSIONS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This repeat comprehensive somatic genomic profiling of a hepatic metastasis
from a patient with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer reveals
a complex and evolving genomic landscape consistent with multi-mechanism
treatment resistance. The most clinically significant new findings are:

1. AR PATHWAY RE-ACTIVATION AS PRIMARY OLAPARIB RESISTANCE MECHANISM:
   Newly acquired AR amplification (high-level, CN 12) combined with a
   de novo AR ligand-binding domain mutation (p.L702H) and detectable
   AR-V7 splice variant mRNA collectively indicate that AR pathway
   re-engagement — rather than loss of BRCA2 function — is the dominant
   driver of progression on olaparib. This is consistent with emerging
   data demonstrating that AR signaling can provide a survival bypass
   to PARP-inhibitor-induced lethality in HRD-positive prostate cancer.
   The AR p.L702H mutation is of particular interest as it confers
   agonist activity to glucocorticoids (including prednisone, which this
   patient received concurrently during abiraterone therapy); it also
   reduces enzalutamide binding affinity.

2. BRCA2 BIALLELIC LOSS RETAINED WITHOUT REVERSION:
   The pathogenic BRCA2 germline frameshift mutation with somatic LOH is
   fully retained at high clonal fraction and no reversion mutations are
   present, indicating that homologous recombination deficiency persists.
   This is relevant for future platinum-based therapy (carboplatin) and
   potentially for re-challenge strategies or novel PARP-trapping approaches
   should AR co-targeting be employed.

3. COMPLETE RB1 LOSS AND NEUROENDOCRINE SUSCEPTIBILITY:
   The progression from heterozygous to homozygous RB1 deletion, in the
   context of concurrent TP53 gain-of-function mutation, constitutes the
   canonical genomic substrate for treatment-emergent neuroendocrine
   prostate cancer (t-NEPC). This finding is concordant with the
   histopathologic detection of focal neuroendocrine differentiation
   on the concurrent liver biopsy and with elevated serum chromogranin A
   and NSE, supporting a transition toward a mixed adenocarcinoma/NEPC
   phenotype and reinforcing the rationale for platinum/etoposide-based
   chemotherapy.

4. CONVERGING PI3K PATHWAY DYSREGULATION:
   The combination of pre-existing PTEN homozygous deletion and newly
   acquired PIK3CA p.H1047R creates dual convergent activation of the
   PI3K/AKT/mTOR axis. This pathway is an established co-driver of
   castration resistance and may represent a future therapeutic target,
   particularly in investigational combinations with hormonal therapies.

5. HIGH-LEVEL MYC AND CDK4 AMPLIFICATION:
   The dramatic increase in MYC copy number (CN 6 → 18) reflects
   progressive genomic instability and aggressive clonal evolution.
   MYC-driven transcriptional reprogramming may facilitate neuroendocrine
   lineage plasticity and metabolic adaptation to androgen-deprived
   conditions.

Overall, this genomic profile depicts a highly evolved, genomically
instable mCRPC with multi-pathway resistance, emerging neuroendocrine
transdifferentiation, and retained HRD — features that collectively
portend a challenging clinical course requiring multimodal therapeutic
strategy.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPORT ATTESTATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This report was reviewed and electronically signed by a board-certified
molecular pathologist and reviewed by the clinical genomics team.
Variant classification follows ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines and ClinGen/CGC/
ViCC somatic variant classification framework (2022).
All variants reported at VAF ≥5% and supported by ≥10 unique supporting
reads unless otherwise indicated.
This report has been generated in accordance with CAP/CLIA laboratory
accreditation standards.

Laboratory Director:      [Laboratory Director, MD, PhD], FCAP
Molecular Pathologist:    [Reviewing Pathologist, MD]
Report Version:           Final
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
